title: A THOUSAND FUNERALS
content:
A THOUSAND FUNERALS
I saw a post that said,
"How did you survive that?"
"I didn't."
"I'm someone else now."
And I took a deep breath
My heart ached
And tears stung my eyes remembering all the versions of me that I laid to rest
A thousand funerals for parts of me
I so desperately loved
And so desperately wanted to keep
1 buried the girl who trusted first.
Who believed
that if she loved people enough, they would choose not to hurt her.
I buried the girl who apologized before she even knew what she'd done wrong.
The one who thought kindness could fix cruelty.
The one who didnt know anger,
Who couldn't imagine raising her voice, even to protect herself.
Everyone said 1 was naive, that I wouldn't survive this world
They were right.
I didn't.
Now I'm rough around the edges
Ilock every door
before I let someone in.
I'm the angry oldest daughter
An untouched grenade that can go off any second Almost itching for a fight
Sometimes I catch myself missing the girl 1 was so fiercely
it steals the air from my lungs.
1 miss how easily she laughed.
How lightly she carried herself.
How she walked into rooms expecting warmth instead of danger.
But she paid for that softness with pieces of herself.
I will never ask her to pay that price again.
So now I'm the kind of woman who stands between her and everything that taught her she had to disappear.
